title: cyperus mollipes var. globifer
intro: Cyperus mollipes var. globifer, also known as the globular-topped umbrella sedge, is a plant species that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae) and is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cyperus mollipes var. globifer include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by drying and crushing the leaves and stems, which can then be infused in hot water to create a tea or used as a topical application.
